# Team News: Uruguay vs. Cape Verde injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Lewis Nolan| 20 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 21 Jun 2026 01:45

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Uruguay could be the next giants that Cape Verde take points from at the 2026 World Cup when they clash on Sunday at Miami Stadium.

It would be foolish if Uruguay took victory against Cape Verde for granted after their opponents held Spain to a goalless stalemate last time out.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## URUGUAY vs. CAPE VERDE

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/uruguay/world-cup-2026/preview/uruguay-vs-cape-verde-prediction-team-news-lineups_599604.html

## URUGUAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Ronald Araujo(calf), Giorgian de Arrascaeta (calf), Jose Gimenez(fitness)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Muslera; Varela, Caceres, Olivera, Sanabria; Valverde, Bentancur, Ugarte, Araujo; Canobbio, Nunez

## CAPE VERDE

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Vozinha; Moreira, Borges, Lop

# Uruguay vs Cape Verde Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group H

Uruguay vs Cape Verde clash in Miami for their pivotal 2026 World Cup Group H showdown. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

# Uruguay vs Cape Verde Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group H

Uruguay needed a Maximiliano Araujo goal to rescue a 1-1 draw with Saudi Arabia, a flat opener for a side many fancy to go deep. Cape Verde, the tournament's smallest nation, wrote history of their own, holding European champions Spain to a goalless draw behind a heroic display from 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha. Both arrive at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami on a point, and both will see this as the game that shapes their 2026 World Cup Group H.

Here is the thing that should worr

# Uruguay lineup vs. Cape Verde: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 Group H game amid Ronald Araujo injury fears

By Lewis Nolan| 19 Jun 2026 22:38, Last updated: 19 Jun 2026 22:46

© Iconsport / PA Images

Uruguay centre-back Ronald Araujo at serious risk of missing out on his nation's clash with Cape Verde in the World Cup on Sunday.

The defender was included in the squad despite picking up an injury before the start of the tournament, and while he has not yet been ruled out of the weekend's game, it would be surprising if he was selected from the start of the match.

In his absence, expect to see a central pairing of Sebastian Caceres and Mathias Olivera, with the duo set to protect goalkeeper Fernando Muslera.

Guillermo Varela is the most likely candidate to start on the right side of defence, while Juan Manuel Sanabria will be considered for a role on the left side.

Manuel Ugarte is Marcelo Bielsa's best ball winner, but he struggled in Uruguay's opening round against Saudi Arabia, so he will need to improve signi

news_items.title: Uruguay v Cape Verde Preview: Heroic Blue Sharks take on two time winners - VAVEL International
news_items.author: Kieran Gallop
news_items.text:
Uruguay v Cape Verde Preview: Heroic Blue Sharks take on two time winners - VAVEL International

GroupA

GroupB

GroupC

GroupD

GroupE

GroupF

GroupG

GroupH

GroupI

GroupJ

GroupK

GroupL

It will be the first time that these sides face off against each other when they meet in Miami. (Photo by Franck FIFE and Arisson MARINHO / AFP via Getty Images)

Marcelo Bielsa’s side were sluggish in the first half, and struggled to get going before Maxi Araujo equalised with ten minutes to spare.

Meanwhile, 40 year old Vozinha put up a stellar performance to help repel the European Champions, capturing the hearts of millions around the world in the process.

With all four teams in Group H now on one point, this game poses a monumental opportunity for both sides to take huge strides to qualification for the knockout stages, with a win potentially confirming progression.

## Team News:

Uruguay -

Bielsa will still be without Barcelona’s Ronald Araujo, who is still carrying a calf problem. Likewise, Giorgian De Arrascaeta will miss out with the same injury.

Atletico Madrid’s Jose Gimenez is a doubt.

Cape Verde -

Former Sporting starlet Jovane Cabral is questionable for this fixture after

By Lewis Nolan| 20 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 21 Jun 2026 01:45

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Uruguay could be the next giants that Cape Verde take points from at the 2026 World Cup when they clash on Sunday at Miami Stadium.

## URUGUAY vs. CAPE VERDE

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/uruguay/world-cup-2026/preview/uruguay-vs-cape-verde-prediction-team-news-lineups_599604.html

Uruguay centre-back Ronald Araujo at serious risk of missing out on his nation's clash with Cape Verde in the World Cup on Sunday.

The defender was included in the squad despite picking up an injury before the start of the tournament, and while he has not yet been ruled out of the weekend's game, it would be surprising if he was selected from the start of the match.

In his absence, expect to see a central pairing of Sebastian Caceres and Mathias Olivera, with the duo set to protect goalkeeper Fernando Muslera.

Guillermo Varela is the most likely candidate to start on the right side of defence, while Juan Manuel Sanabria will be considered for a role on the left side.
